signal_title: Propuesta de Ley Federal de Comercio Electrónico
risk_rationale: The draft bill proposes new regulations for digital commerce in Mexico, focusing on consumer rights and cybersecurity. While the exact requirements are not specified, these areas align with existing e-commerce and data security policies, suggesting moderate risk if new specific requirements are introduced.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Return policy minimum: 7-day no-questions-asked (14 in EU)
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Potential gap if new consumer rights exceed current return policy standards.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: EU-US transfers: EU-US Data Privacy Framework + SCCs
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Potential gap if new cybersecurity measures are mandated beyond current data transfer and protection protocols.

remediations.title: Evaluate and Enhance Consumer Rights Compliance
remediations.description: Conduct a detailed analysis of the proposed consumer rights consolidation to identify specific areas where current policies may fall short. Prepare to adjust return policies and other consumer rights-related features to align with new regulations.
acceptance_criteria: Updated return policy to meet new legal standards

remediations.title: Strengthen Cybersecurity Measures
remediations.description: Review current cybersecurity protocols and compare them against the proposed requirements. Enhance data protection and cybersecurity measures to ensure compliance with potential new standards.

business_impact: Moderate impact expected due to potential need for policy adjustments and enhancements in consumer rights and cybersecurity measures.
recommended_actions: Monitor the progress of the draft bill and any amendments.
Engage with local legal experts to interpret the potential impact of the bill.
Prepare contingency plans for rapid compliance adjustments if the bill is enacted.
